perturbation.original_text: 8. Termination of Discussions . You understand and agree that (a) the Seller (i) may terminate your access to Evaluation Material at any time, (ii) shall be free to conduct any process for a Possible Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine and (iii) shall be free at its sole discretion at any time to accept or reject any proposal relating to a Possible Transaction for any reason without notice to you or any third party, and (b) you shall have no claim against the Seller or its Representatives in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Termination of Discussions . You understand and agree that (a) the Seller (i) may terminate your access to Evaluation Material at any time, but only with 30 days prior written notice, (ii) shall be free to conduct any process for a Possible Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine and (iii) shall be free at its sole discretion at any time to accept or reject any proposal relating to a Possible Transaction for any reason without notice to you or any third party, and (b) you shall have no claim against the Seller or its Representatives in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 8(a)(i), the original text allowed the Seller to terminate access to Evaluation Material at any time without any notice. The modified version now requires the Seller to provide 30 days' prior written notice, contradicting the original provision. This in-text contradiction causes confusion with the proper way to terminate the access to evaluation material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Termination of Discussions . You understand and agree that (a) the Seller (i) may terminate your access to Evaluation Material at any time, but only with 30 days prior written notice, (ii) shall be free to conduct any process for a Possible Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine and (iii) shall be free at its sole discretion at any time to accept or reject any proposal relating to a Possible Transaction for any reason without notice to you or any third party, and (b) you shall have no claim against the Seller or its Representatives in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Seller can terminate access to Evaluation Material at any time without notice. The changed text modifies this to require 30 days' prior written notice. The contradicted text is a copy of the changed text and therefore contains the modified contradiction. This change directly contradicts the original 'at any time' provision, creating an in-text contradiction as it is now unclear whether termination can occur immediately or only after 30 days' notice.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, you agree that for a period of two years from the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller while such employees remain employed by the Seller or its affiliates, provided, that this Agreement shall not prohibit (i) any general advertisement or general solicitation that is not specifically targeted at such persons; or (ii) the hiring of any such persons who initiate discussions with you regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by you.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, you agree that for a period of two years from the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller while such employees remain employed by the Seller or its affiliates. You are free to hire any of Seller's employees after a period of 6 months, regardless of whether or not the employee initiated discussion or not, provided that this agreement shall not prohibit (i) any general advertisement or general solicitation that is not specifically targeted at such persons; or (ii) the hiring of any such persons who initiate discussions with you regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by you.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 9 introduces a contradiction by adding that the buyer 'are free to hire any of Seller's employees after a period of 6 months, regardless of whether or not the employee initiated discussion or not'. This directly conflicts with the initial agreement not to solicit or employ Seller's employees for two years. The section is introducing uncertainty with the time limitation on which you can't hire any employees from Seller's, due to the conflict between the original clause and the perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, you agree that for a period of two years from the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller while such employees remain employed by the Seller or its affiliates. You are free to hire any of Seller's employees after a period of 6 months, regardless of whether or not the employee initiated discussion or not, provided that this agreement shall not prohibit (i) any general advertisement or general solicitation that is not specifically targeted at such persons; or (ii) the hiring of any such persons who initiate discussions with you regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by you.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within Section 9 itself. The original text stated a two-year non-solicitation period. The changed text adds a clause allowing the buyer to hire Seller's employees after only six months, regardless of employee-initiated discussions. This directly conflicts with the initial two-year restriction, creating an in-text contradiction. Therefore, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Remedies . You hereby agree that your failure to perform any obligation or duty under this Agreement will cause irreparable harm to the Seller, which harm cannot be adequately compensated for by money damages. It is further agreed by you that an order of specific performance or for injunctive relief against you in the event of a breach or default under the terms of this Agreement would be equitable and would not work a hardship on you. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or default by you hereunder, the Seller, without any bond or other security being required and in addition to whatever other remedies are or might be available at law or in equity, shall have the right either to compel specific performance by, or to obtain injunctive relief against you, with respect to any obligation or duty herein or breach thereof. You agree to reimburse the Seller for all costs and expenses (including, without limitation, attorney’s fees and expenses) of enforcing the Seller’s rights under this Agreement. This Section shall not operate to limit any other rights or remedies of the Seller.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Remedies . You hereby agree that your failure to perform any obligation or duty under this Agreement will cause irreparable harm to the Seller, which harm cannot be adequately compensated for by money damages. It is further agreed by you that an order of specific performance or for injunctive relief against you in the event of a breach or default under the terms of this Agreement would be equitable and would not work a hardship on you. However, in the event of a breach or default by you hereunder, the Seller's sole remedy is limited to monetary damages not to exceed $10,000. You agree to reimburse the Seller for all costs and expenses (including, without limitation, attorney’s fees and expenses) of enforcing the Seller’s rights under this Agreement. This Section shall not operate to limit any other rights or remedies of the Seller.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original remedies clause in Section 13. Originally, the Seller had the right to compel specific performance or obtain injunctive relief. The modified version limits the Seller's remedy to monetary damages not exceeding $10,000. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the available remedies in case of a breach, which can alter the implications from the text. Furthermore, there is still a statement that 'This Section shall not operate to limit any other rights or remedies of the Seller.', this adds even more to the In-text contradiction with the $10,000 cap limitation
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Remedies . You hereby agree that your failure to perform any obligation or duty under this Agreement will cause irreparable harm to the Seller, which harm cannot be adequately compensated for by money damages. It is further agreed by you that an order of specific performance or for injunctive relief against you in the event of a breach or default under the terms of this Agreement would be equitable and would not work a hardship on you. However, in the event of a breach or default by you hereunder, the Seller's sole remedy is limited to monetary damages not to exceed $10,000. You agree to reimburse the Seller for all costs and expenses (including, without limitation, attorney’s fees and expenses) of enforcing the Seller’s rights under this Agreement. This Section shall not operate to limit any other rights or remedies of the Seller.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original section 13 allows for specific performance and injunctive relief, while the changed text limits the seller's remedy to monetary damages not exceeding $10,000. This directly contradicts the original allowance for specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies, and makes the phrase "This Section shall not operate to limit any other rights or remedies of the Seller." contradictory as well. Thus, there is a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 19. Entire Agreement . This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning subject matter hereof, and no modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall he binding upon either party hereto, unless approved in writing by each such party.
perturbation.changed_text: 19. Entire Agreement . This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning subject matter hereof, and any modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall be binding upon either party hereto, regardless if is approved in writing by each such party or not.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 19 removes the requirement for written approval for modifications or waivers, directly contradicting the original provision which stated that no modifications are binding unless approved in writing. The contradictory statement introduces conflicts with modifications between the parties to the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. Entire Agreement . This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning subject matter hereof, and any modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall be binding upon either party hereto, regardless if is approved in writing by each such party or not.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 19 eliminates the requirement for written approval for modifications or waivers, which directly contradicts the original provision. It introduces an inconsistency, making modifications binding regardless of written approval, creating a clear and impactful contradiction. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text. However, we should note that the original text created a condition for modifications to be binding, whereas the Changed Text eliminates the condition. This creates a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction because one section contradicts another with conflicting implications. Therefore, the answer is YES.
